description: Minimal trigger for the production trio (FEAT-010): a Django management command planner/management/commands/run_trio.py that invokes the existing planner.orchestration.run_trio_to_proposed_plan(school, author, draft_id, seed, max_rounds) -> (Plan, residual) and reports the result. Mirrors the house command pattern (school/management/commands/import_responsibility_atoms.py): add_arguments (--school-slug default chiway-repton-xiamen resolving School.objects.get(slug=...); --author-email resolving the User to stamp plan.author, falling back to a superuser; --seed; --max-rounds; --dry-run) + handle (resolve school+author with CommandError on failure; mint draft_id=uuid4; run inside transaction.atomic with set_rollback on dry-run; catch the no-active-cycle ValidationError -> CommandError; report via self.stdout/self.style the Plan pk/title/lifecycle, the residual gate-failure messages, and the MUST_FIX plan.flags). Create the planner/management + commands package __init__.py (planner has no management dir). THIN trigger only -- no new orchestration/gate/promote/Flag logic, no lifecycle flip (admin alone promotes via the existing advance_plan_status_action), no view/admin action (the streaming planner view is a separate later slice). Realizes the trio's user-invocability; unblocks the post-Phase-15 end-to-end walkthrough + the US-LLM-23/24/25 vivified flips. Addresses FEAT-010; harness SLICE=trio_command.
acceptance_criteria: Invoking the command for a school that has an active cycle, with an author, runs the trio end-to-end and persists a Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED) owned by that school and author; the command reports the saved Plan's identity (pk/title/lifecycle), the residual gate-failure messages, and the persisted MUST_FIX flags.

A precondition failure -- a missing/unknown school, an unresolvable author, or a school with no active cycle -- exits with a clear CommandError and persists NO Plan (never a raw traceback, never a partial Plan).
--dry-run runs the trio but persists no Plan (the work is rolled back); the command still reports what it would have produced.

The command is a thin trigger: it adds no orchestration/gate/promote/Flag logic of its own (it invokes the existing run_trio_to_proposed_plan and reads back the returned Plan/residual), does not flip plan.lifecycle, adds no view/admin action, and modifies none of the trio internals.
The whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates.
